questions.stem: Which Excel tool finds the single input value needed to make a formula cell reach a target result?
questions.explanation: Goal Seek works backward from a desired formula result to find the one input value that produces it, which is the core idea of this kind of what-if analysis.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Under which Ribbon tab are Goal Seek, Data Table and Scenario Manager grouped?
questions.explanation: All three appear under the Data tab in the What-If Analysis drop-down menu within the Forecast group.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The Goal Seek dialog box has three boxes. Which one must contain a formula?
questions.explanation: The Set cell box must point to a cell containing a formula, because Goal Seek adjusts the input until that formula equals the target typed in the To value box.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How many input cells can Goal Seek change at one time?
questions.explanation: Goal Seek adjusts only a single changing cell. Problems that need several inputs to change together call for Solver instead.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Cell A2 contains the formula =A1*5. You run Goal Seek to set A2 to 60 by changing A1. What value does A1 become?
questions.explanation: Goal Seek solves A1*5 = 60, so A1 becomes 60 divided by 5, which is 12.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Cell B3 contains =B1*B2 and B2 equals 4. You run Goal Seek to set B3 to 100 by changing B1. What value does B1 become?
questions.explanation: With B2 fixed at 4, Goal Seek solves B1*4 = 100, so B1 becomes 100 divided by 4, which is 25.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A profit cell holds =price*100 - 2000, where price is the changing cell. Goal Seek sets profit to 0. What break-even price does it find?
questions.explanation: Setting price*100 - 2000 = 0 gives price*100 = 2000, so the break-even price is 2000 divided by 100, which is 20.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which what-if tool shows how a formula result changes across a whole list of input values at once?
questions.explanation: A Data Table substitutes a series of input values into a model and lays out the corresponding formula results, letting you see many outcomes in a single grid.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How many input cells does a one-variable data table use?
questions.explanation: A one-variable data table feeds a series of values into a single input cell, either the row input cell or the column input cell, but not both.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A two-variable data table requires you to specify which two settings?
options: A row input cell and a column input cell
questions.explanation: A two-variable data table substitutes one series of values through the row input cell and another series through the column input cell, so both must be supplied.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In a two-variable data table, where must the single formula being analysed be placed?
options: In the top-left corner cell of the table
questions.explanation: A two-variable data table allows only one formula, and it must sit in the cell at the intersection of the row of inputs and the column of inputs, the top-left corner of the table body.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: After a data table is created, what kind of result does Excel place in the body cells?
options: An array formula that cannot be edited cell by cell
questions.explanation: Excel fills the body of a data table with a single array formula based on the TABLE function. You cannot edit or delete one result on its own, only the whole table.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which tool lets you save several named sets of input values and switch between them?
questions.explanation: Scenario Manager stores named groups of input values, such as best case and worst case, and lets you display each set in turn by choosing Show.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In Scenario Manager, what do the changing cells represent?
options: The input cells whose values differ between scenarios
questions.explanation: The changing cells are the input cells that take different values from one scenario to the next, while the formulas that depend on them recalculate automatically.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of changing cells a single scenario in Scenario Manager can hold?
questions.explanation: Scenario Manager allows up to 32 changing cells in any one scenario, which is enough for most planning models.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which output does Scenario Manager produce to compare all stored scenarios side by side?
questions.explanation: The Summary button in Scenario Manager generates a Scenario Summary report on a new sheet, listing each scenario with its changing cells and result cells together.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A loan payment model has many inputs, and you need the best combination of rate and term to hit a target payment. Which tool is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Goal Seek changes only one cell, so a problem with two or more inputs adjusted together is suited to Solver, the add-in built for multi-variable optimisation with constraints.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Goal Seek reaches its answer by which method?
options: Iteration, trying values until the result is close enough
Random guessing with no stopping rule
questions.explanation: Goal Seek uses iteration, repeatedly adjusting the input and recalculating until the formula result is within a small tolerance of the target, so the answer can be a close approximation.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: For Goal Seek to work, the changing cell must contain what?
options: A constant value, not a formula
questions.explanation: The changing cell must hold a plain value rather than a formula, because Goal Seek overwrites that cell while searching. The formula belongs in the Set cell.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When you click OK on a successful Goal Seek, what happens to the worksheet?
options: The changing cell keeps the solved value
questions.explanation: Choosing OK accepts the result, so the changing cell keeps the value Goal Seek found. Choosing Cancel instead would restore the original input.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A one-variable data table lists candidate interest rates down a column and shows monthly payments beside them. What goes in the cell just above and to the right of the input column, at the head of the results?
options: A reference to the formula that computes the payment
questions.explanation: In a column-oriented one-variable data table, the formula or a reference to it is placed one row up and one column to the right of the top input value, so Excel knows which result to compute for each rate.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which statement about a two-variable data table is correct?
options: It can analyse only one formula
It can analyse several formulas at once
questions.explanation: A two-variable data table is limited to a single formula placed in its corner cell. To study more than one output you would build several tables or use a one-variable table, which can show multiple result columns.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the main advantage of Scenario Manager over simply retyping inputs by hand?
options: It stores each set of inputs so you can recall any of them without re-entering values
It prevents the workbook from saving
It runs faster than the worksheet
questions.explanation: Scenario Manager remembers every named set of input values, so you can switch among them or summarise them at any time without manually re-entering each combination.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible
